cases.name: serialized_position_allocation
cases.expected: transactional ActiveModel comment inserts lock the tenant thread row before reading the highest position and overwrite caller-supplied position with the next checked value

cases.name: exact_active_comment_count
cases.expected: explicit transactional counter refreshes lock the tenant thread row and replace caller-supplied comment_count with the number of tenant comments whose deleted_at is null

cases.name: status_only_update_preserves_count
cases.expected: thread status, timestamp, and metadata updates that do not explicitly set comment_count leave the stored counter unchanged

cases.name: historical_counter_repair
cases.expected: the append-only migration recalculates all stored thread counters from active owner rows for PostgreSQL and SQLite

cases.name: historical_position_repair
cases.expected: the migration deterministically ranks existing comments by position, creation time, and id within each thread before adding the uniqueness constraint

cases.name: bulk_bypass_rejection
cases.expected: a unique thread_id plus position index rejects direct Entity insert paths that bypass ActiveModelBehavior

cases.name: postgres_concurrent_create_delete
cases.expected: two independent PostgreSQL connections preserve positions 1..3 and an exact active count across concurrent create/create and create/soft-delete phases

runtime_promotion_requirements: execute the env-gated PostgreSQL case with RUSTOK_COMMENTS_TEST_DATABASE_URL
retain final active-row count and gap-free unique position evidence
retain migration output for a fixture containing stale counters and duplicate positions
